data.label: Move (trajectory action + live twin stream)
data.description: ROS `send_action_goal` to `/xarm6_traj_controller/follow_joint_trajectory` (`control_msgs/action/FollowJointTrajectory`). Commands a multi-waypoint out-and-back trajectory home → [0, -0.3, -0.3, 0, 0.6, 0] → home over ~9 s on the xArm 6 driven through ros2_control fake hardware (reuses demo 32's exact `interface_typedefs` and deployment — capacity `xarm_fleet`, requirement `ros.robot_model == xarm6` — verbatim). This node ALSO declares a Data/Out channel `joints`: as the controller follows the trajectory the ROS backend streams its ACTUAL FollowJointTrajectory feedback (`actual.positions`) as NDJSON joint-state frames `{"joint_names":[joint1..joint6],"positions":[6 floats]}` onto the channel, so the UI render registry (planLiveRender) routes the stream to the robot-arm twin renderer and animates a live 3D digital twin of the REAL motion on the `move` → `twin_view` graph edge — NO re-computed/interpolated trajectory; the twin reflects the arm's true joint states. The element content_type is `application/vnd.aithericon.joint-state+x-ndjson;model=xarm6`. The bulk joint stream rides the out-of-band JetStream datastream transport — it NEVER enters the net marking (open + close only). The terminal action RESULT (`error_code`) rides `stdout_tail` ONLY — success is implicit (the backend only succeeds when GoalStatus == 4 SUCCEEDED). The goal is authored as free-form JSON in `config.fields` and passed to rosbridge verbatim. Runs on an `xarm_fleet` runner that satisfies `ros.robot_model == xarm6`; the rosbridge endpoint is runner-local (`EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL`).

data.label: Twin view (drain joint stream)
data.description: Consumer of the `joints` data channel — the net-side anchor the live 3D URDF twin renders ON (the UI taps the SAME channel out-of-band via `?follow=1`, while this node drains it on the data plane). Reads the NDJSON joint-state frames with the ordinary transport-unaware `for chunk in stream('joints')` API, reframes on newlines and counts complete frames. The robot model is carried in the channel content_type as `;model=xarm6` so the twin renderer knows which URDF (`robot_description` asset, ref_key xarm6) to load. The frames carry the REAL arm's ACTUAL joint positions streamed live from the `move` node's FollowJointTrajectory feedback. The bulk stream rides the out-of-band JetStream datastream transport — the net sees only open + close.
